text.title: PLG Agentic Workflow and Failure Modes
text.summary: Describes the weekly PM-driven PLG loop, PQL hand-off contract structure, agent-specific gotchas, and the hand-off artifacts between PM and growth.

section.title: Weekly PLG Loop

rule.statement: Run three subagent passes per week: analytics pass, discovery pass, and SDD planning pass.
rule.rationale: PLG is a metric-driven loop, not a feature-shipping cadence. Weekly cadence prevents metric drift and ensures activation signals translate into sprint tasks within one week.

section.p: Analytics pass: pull activation/retention/expansion cohorts from PostHog/Amplitude/Mixpanel, diff vs. 4-week median, flag metrics more than 1 standard deviation below baseline. Discovery pass: synthesize activation drop-off users into 5 friction themes with hypotheses. Planning pass: convert top friction finding into a todo/ SDD task.

rule.statement: Hold a weekly 30-minute PLG review with growth: PM + growth + analytics + 1 engineer. No slides; read the snapshot file. Decide one experiment to ship.
rule.rationale: Experiment velocity, not experiment quality, is the primary PLG growth lever once the funnel is instrumented.

section.title: PQL Hand-off Contract

rule.statement: Each PQL has exactly one PM owner and one growth/sales owner; two PMs splitting a PQL means nobody acts.
rule.rationale: Split ownership causes PQL scoring drift and silent deal loss between three systems: product analytics, CRM, and the PQL definition file.

rule.statement: Route PQL-to-sales hand-off via webhook to CRM SDR queue, not via Slack message.
rule.rationale: Slack pings rot; a PQL firing to a CRM webhook creates a durable, auditable record the sales team acts on within a defined SLA.

section.reference: PQL YAML spec — the contract file both PM and growth co-sign on any change

antipattern.description: Redefining "activation" every quarter without a frozen written definition tied to D30 retention.
antipattern.reason: A metric that changes definition each quarter is decorative; the trend line means nothing because the denominator changed.

antipattern.description: Vanity North Star: "Weekly active users" without a revenue link.
antipattern.reason: Engagement hacks (notifications, streaks) move WAU while harming D30 retention; the metric rewards the wrong behavior.

antipattern.description: Optimizing TTV to under 5 minutes without measuring D7 retention (the "time-to-second-value" cliff).
antipattern.reason: A fast first run that does not lead to a second session has not created a retained user; D1-to-D7 conversion is the actual growth lever once TTV is acceptable.

antipattern.description: PLG without a content/SEO acquisition funnel — shipping features instead of fixing empty top-of-funnel.
antipattern.reason: PLG is not acquisition; without inbound traffic the activation funnel optimizes an empty pipe.

section.title: Agent Gotchas

rule.statement: Force every analytics agent to print the query it ran and the row count it received before any commentary.
rule.rationale: Agents confidently restate activation-rate numbers without checking time windows; requiring the SQL/HogQL output catches window mismatches before they reach the PM.

rule.statement: Segment cohorts by persona (developer vs. designer vs. PM) before reporting; reject single-cohort outputs.
rule.rationale: Persona collapse makes activation look uniform when it is segmented; a funnel optimal for developers may be hostile to designers signing up with the same product.

rule.statement: Agents must NOT autonomously change paywall placement, free-tier limits, or pricing — those are revenue-impacting governance decisions requiring human-in-the-loop approval.
rule.rationale: Autonomous pricing changes by agents are a category of irreversible harm; the PM and growth owner must both approve any paywall or limit change.

section.reference: Weekly PLG metric snapshot script using PostHog/HogQL
